name: Audit Existing Scripts
description: Review existing discovery and flash scripts/tools. Identify what already works, what needs modification, and what gaps exist.
objective: Complete understanding of existing tooling: what is parsed, what output format is used, what is missing, and what modifications are needed.

tasks.name: Review existing discovery scripts
tasks.description: Identify what is already parsed, including device types, output format, model fields, and firmware fields. Document current capabilities.

tasks.name: Review existing flash scripts
tasks.description: Identify existing flash tools and workflows. Document supported components, commands used, and safety checks already present.

tasks.name: Identify gaps vs firmware table requirements
tasks.description: Compare existing script output with firmware table requirements: component type, model, vendor, firmware version, and device path for each permanent component type.

tasks.name: Test existing scripts on current hardware
tasks.description: Run existing scripts on available lab hardware. Capture current output and identify what works, what fails, and what requires privilege handling.

name: Modify Discovery Scripts
description: Modify existing discovery scripts to output structured JSON with model and firmware version per permanent device.
objective: Discovery script outputs JSON for permanent devices and handles missing tools gracefully.

tasks.name: Modify scripts to output structured JSON
tasks.description: Adapt discovery scripts to output consistent JSON records with type, model, vendor, firmware, and device_path fields.

tasks.name: Add missing device parsers
tasks.description: Add parsing for missing permanent component types identified during audit. Handle absent vendor tools without crashing.

tasks.name: Test modified scripts on lab hardware
tasks.description: Run modified scripts on lab systems. Validate JSON output and confirm expected device types are discovered correctly.

name: Manifest and Repo Infrastructure
description: Define the firmware manifest schema, create an initial manifest, and host it under the 45Drives repository infrastructure.
objective: Manifest is hosted at repo.45drives.com/firmware/manifest.json with latest validated firmware versions for supported models.

tasks.name: Define manifest.json schema
tasks.description: Finalize manifest structure, including schema version, component categories, model matching, latest firmware, flash metadata, release dates, and release notes.

tasks.name: Populate initial manifest with test system devices
tasks.description: Create initial manifest entries for device models found on test systems and research current firmware versions from vendor sources.

tasks.name: Host manifest on repo.45drives.com
tasks.description: Add a firmware path to repository hosting, upload manifest.json, and verify it is accessible over HTTPS.

name: Firmware Check Cache Service
description: Create a backend check command that runs discovery, fetches the manifest, compares versions, and writes a local cache for the UI.
objective: A reliable root-run service writes /var/cache/45drives/firmware.json with discovery and update status data.

tasks.name: Implement firmware-check command
tasks.description: Create a command that runs discovery, fetches the hosted manifest, compares versions, and builds a cache structure.

tasks.name: Add robust version comparison
tasks.description: Normalize common version formats and classify devices as current, outdated, or unknown.

tasks.name: Write cache atomically
tasks.description: Write cache data to a temporary file and replace the target cache file atomically to avoid partial reads by the UI.

tasks.name: Add systemd service and timer
tasks.description: Install a oneshot service and daily timer to refresh firmware status with randomized delay and persistent catch-up behavior.

name: UI Integration
description: Display firmware status in the 45drives-system UI using cached backend data.
objective: Administrators can see current and latest firmware versions, update state, and basic firmware metadata from the Cockpit UI.

tasks.name: Load firmware cache from UI
tasks.description: Read /var/cache/45drives/firmware.json from Cockpit with required privileges and handle missing cache data gracefully.

tasks.name: Merge firmware status into PCI table
tasks.description: Match cache devices against PCI entries and show latest firmware plus update state in the existing PCI table.

tasks.name: Display BIOS and BMC firmware status
tasks.description: Surface BIOS and BMC current firmware, latest firmware, and update state in the system section.

tasks.name: Add update state badges
tasks.description: Use compact status labels for current, outdated, and unknown firmware comparison states.

tasks.name: Handle cache loading and empty states
tasks.description: Ensure the UI remains usable when cache data is unavailable, malformed, or missing individual component entries.

tasks.name: Test UI with representative cache fixtures
tasks.description: Test current, outdated, unknown, and missing-cache states with representative cache JSON samples.

name: Firmware Flash Capability
description: Phase 2: add controlled firmware flash workflows after display-only status is stable.
objective: Administrators can start supported firmware updates with clear warnings, preflight checks, and auditable results.

tasks.name: Define flash safety requirements
tasks.description: Document required preflight checks, supported tools, reboot handling, user confirmations, and failure states.

tasks.name: Implement backend flash runner
tasks.description: Add a privileged backend command that validates the manifest entry, selects the supported flash tool, runs the update, and captures logs.

tasks.name: Add UI flash action flow
tasks.description: Expose flash actions only for supported devices and require explicit confirmation before starting an update.

tasks.name: Record flash results
tasks.description: Persist update results, command output, timestamps, and reboot requirements so administrators can audit the operation.

tasks.name: Test flash flow on supported hardware
tasks.description: Validate safe behavior on supported lab hardware, including successful updates, blocked unsupported updates, and failure recovery paths.

name: Packaging and Release
description: Package firmware discovery scripts, cache service files, and timer units with the cockpit hardware modules.
objective: Fresh installs and upgrades include the firmware scripts, systemd units, dependencies, and enabled timer behavior.

tasks.name: Update RPM and DEB spec files
tasks.description: Add new scripts and systemd unit files to packaging specs. Add required package dependencies.

tasks.name: Test clean install on fresh system
tasks.description: Install on fresh Rocky 9 and Ubuntu Jammy systems. Verify timer activation, cache generation, and UI behavior.

tasks.name: Test upgrade from previous version
tasks.description: Upgrade from a previous cockpit-45drives-hardware version. Verify new files are added, timer starts, and no conflicts are introduced.

name: HDD Firmware Update
description: Integrate HDD firmware flashing using existing SeaChest tools and discovery parsers. Focus is on testing the update flow end-to-end since tooling already exists.
objective: HDD firmware updates work reliably via SeaChest with LOD files matched from manifest. Validated on Exos 7E10 SATA drives with CRBP-SN04.

tasks.name: Add HDD entries to manifest
tasks.description: Add Exos 7E10 SATA entries to manifest.json with model family matching, LOD binary path (CRBP-SN04), SHA256, and flash tool reference (SeaChest --downloadMicrocode).

tasks.name: Wire existing discovery into firmware-check flow
tasks.description: Ensure existing smartctl/SeaChest discovery output feeds into the firmware-check comparison pipeline. Verify model-to-manifest matching works for HDD entries.

tasks.name: Add safety checks before HDD flash
tasks.description: Verify drive is not in active ZFS scrub or rebuild before flashing. Warn if drive is mounted. Confirm no active I/O before proceeding.

tasks.name: Test single drive flash on Exos 7E10
tasks.description: Flash one Exos 7E10 SATA drive using SeaChest with CRBP-SN04 LOD file. Verify firmware version changes, drive accessible post-flash, no data loss.

tasks.name: Test batch flash across multiple drives
tasks.description: Flash all matching Exos drives in a chassis sequentially. Verify per-drive status tracking, failure on one drive doesn't abort remaining, and all drives report new firmware version.

name: Connected Deployment (Dual-Repo Firmware Sync)
description: Manage firmware files across 2 different repositories (cockpit-hardware and productionScripts). Ensure both repos stay in sync with identical firmware binaries and consistent manifest references.
objective: Firmware files are maintained in a single source of truth and synced to both repos reliably. Adding or updating firmware in one place propagates correctly to the other.

tasks.name: Audit firmware files across both repos
tasks.description: Compare firmware binaries in cockpit-hardware/firmware/ and productionScripts/firmware/. Identify duplicates, version mismatches, and files that exist in only one repo.

tasks.name: Define single source of truth strategy
tasks.description: Decide which repo is authoritative for firmware binaries. Options: one repo owns binaries and the other references via symlink/submodule/script, or shared storage on repo.45drives.com.

tasks.name: Build sync script between repos
tasks.description: Create a script that syncs firmware binaries and manifest references between cockpit-hardware and productionScripts. Verify SHA256 integrity after sync. Report any drift.

tasks.name: Integrate sync into firmware update workflow
tasks.description: When a new firmware file is added or manifest is updated, automatically trigger sync to the other repo. Can be a git hook, CI step, or manual confirmation step.

tasks.name: Test sync with new firmware addition
tasks.description: Add a new firmware file to the source repo, run sync, verify it appears correctly in both repos with matching SHA256 and manifest references.

name: Manifest Management and Process Documentation
description: Create tooling and documentation for efficiently updating the firmware manifest when new firmware versions are released, and document the entire firmware update process end-to-end.
objective: Manifest can be updated quickly via CLI tool or script. Full process is documented so any team member can add new firmware entries, test, and publish.

tasks.name: Build manifest update CLI tool
tasks.description: Create a script/tool that accepts component type, model, new firmware version, binary path, and SHA256 — then updates manifest.json in place with proper validation and formatting.

tasks.name: Add manifest validation and linting
tasks.description: Validate manifest against JSON schema on every update. Check for duplicate entries, missing required fields, invalid SHA256 hashes, and unreachable binary URLs.

tasks.name: Automate SHA256 computation and binary upload
tasks.description: Tool computes SHA256 of firmware binary, uploads to repo.45drives.com/firmware/binaries/, and updates manifest entry with correct path and hash.

tasks.name: Document manifest update process
tasks.description: Write step-by-step guide: how to add a new firmware entry, how to update an existing version, how to test locally, and how to publish to production. Include examples for each device type.

tasks.name: Document end-to-end firmware update process
tasks.description: Write comprehensive documentation covering: architecture overview, discovery flow, manifest schema reference, flash safety model, multi-server deployment, HDD-specific considerations, and troubleshooting guide.

name: Firmware Release Tooling
description: Build internal tooling for 45Drives engineers to efficiently publish firmware updates — updating the manifest, hashing files, signing, and deploying to the repo server. Can be a CLI tool, small web UI, or both.
objective: A streamlined workflow where a release engineer can go from receiving a vendor firmware file to it being live on repo.45drives.com in under 2 minutes with full integrity verification.

tasks.name: Move firmware-release to tools/ directory
tasks.description: Relocate firmware-release and update-manifest-hashes from system_files/ to a tools/ directory that is excluded from customer packaging. These are developer-only utilities.

tasks.name: Decide on UI approach (CLI vs Web UI vs both)
tasks.description: Evaluate whether a CLI tool is sufficient or if a small web UI (drag-drop firmware, pick model, enter version, hit Publish) would be more accessible to non-engineer staff. Consider: who publishes firmware? How often? Technical comfort level?

tasks.name: Implement GPG signing in release workflow
tasks.description: Add GPG manifest signing to the deploy step. Generate 45drives-release GPG key pair. Private key stored on build machine/HSM only. Public key ships with cockpit-hardware package. firmware-release deploy --gpg-key should sign manifest before upload.

tasks.name: Set up repo.45drives.com firmware directory
tasks.description: Configure nginx on repo.45drives.com to serve /firmware/ as a static directory. Set up directory structure: manifest.json, manifest.json.sig, and firmware binaries. Configure HTTPS with valid certificate.

tasks.name: Add GPG verification to firmware-check on customer side
tasks.description: Update firmware-check to verify manifest.json.sig after downloading remote manifest. Reject manifest if signature is invalid or missing. Ship 45drives-release.gpg public key with cockpit-hardware package.

tasks.name: Build web UI for firmware publishing (optional)
tasks.description: Small internal web app (Flask/FastAPI or simple HTML+JS) hosted on build server. Features: drag-drop firmware file, select model from dropdown, enter version, auto-hash, preview diff, one-click deploy. Only accessible on internal network.

tasks.name: End-to-end test: full publish → customer flash cycle
tasks.description: Test complete flow: engineer uses firmware-release to add firmware → deploys to repo → customer machine runs firmware-check (fetches remote manifest, verifies GPG) → firmware-flash downloads file, verifies SHA256, flashes successfully.
